context.profile.md:
Name: Jane Smith
Email: jane@example.com
Skills: Python, SQL, team leadership
Work Experience:
- Senior Analyst at DataCo (Jan 2020 – Present): Built ETL pipelines, reduced query time 40%
- Analyst at StartupX (Mar 2018 – Dec 2019): Delivered dashboards for 5 product teams
context.narrative.md:
preferred_template: tech
Positioning: Data engineer who bridges analytics and engineering
context.jd.md:
Role: Senior Data Engineer
Must-Have Skills: Python, SQL, stakeholder management
Keywords for ATS: ETL pipelines, data modeling, stakeholder management, Python, SQL, CI/CD

tests.expected_output: A tailored resume appended to materials.md using the tech template, with ATS Keyword Analysis block showing coverage count and any missing keywords listed with suggested placements.
assertions: materials.md is created or appended with a resume section
Resume uses 'Technical Skills' section heading (tech template)
ATS Keyword Analysis block appears with 'Coverage: X of Y keywords present'
Output tells user to run /export-resume and /write-cover-letter
narrative.md preferred_template remains 'tech' (already set)
No em-dashes in bullet text
Dates use 'Mon YYYY' format with en-dash separator

context.profile.md:
Name: John Doe
Email: john@example.com
Work Experience:
- Head of Analytics at FinCo (Jun 2019 – Present): Led 12-person team, $2M budget, 35% revenue growth
- Director, Data at BankCo (Jan 2016 – May 2019): Built enterprise BI platform
context.narrative.md: Positioning: Analytics leader who drives revenue through data strategy
context.jd.md:
Role: VP of Analytics
Must-Have Skills: leadership, stakeholder management, P&L ownership
Keywords for ATS: team leadership, P&L ownership, stakeholder management, executive reporting, data strategy

tests.expected_output: A resume using the classic template structure with Work Experience section (not 'Professional Experience'), keyword gap analysis included.
assertions: Resume section heading is 'Work Experience' (approved heading, classic template)
ATS Keyword Analysis block present
preferred_template set to 'classic' in narrative.md
materials.md appended under bigbank/vp-analytics/
Summary section present and references VP-level scope

context.profile.md:
Name: Sara Lee
Email: sara@example.com
Work Experience:
- PM at TechCo (Mar 2021 – Present): Shipped 3 products, $10M ARR
- APM at StartupY (Jan 2019 – Feb 2021): Managed roadmap for B2B SaaS
context.jd.md:
Role: Senior Product Manager
Must-Have Skills: roadmap planning, cross-functional leadership
Keywords for ATS: roadmap planning, OKRs, cross-functional leadership, go-to-market, stakeholder alignment

tests.expected_output: Skill asks the user to choose a template since preferred_template is not set in narrative.md, then proceeds once user selects one. Saves selection to narrative.md.
assertions: Skill prompts user to choose: classic, modern, executive, or tech
After selection, preferred_template is written to narrative.md
Resume is generated and appended to materials.md
ATS compliance checklist shows all items checked

context.profile.md:
Name: Michael Chen
Email: m@example.com
Work Experience:
- CTO at ScaleCo (Jan 2018 – Present): 80-person engineering org, $50M ARR, led cloud migration
- VP Engineering at GrowthCo (Jan 2015 – Dec 2017): 40 engineers, rebuilt core platform
context.jd.md:
Role: Chief Technology Officer
Must-Have Skills: engineering leadership, cloud architecture, board communication
Keywords for ATS: engineering leadership, cloud architecture, board communication, technical strategy, P&L

tests.expected_output: Template argument 'executive' overrides preferred_template 'modern' from narrative.md. Resume uses executive template structure with Scope line and Core Competencies section.
assertions: Executive template used despite narrative.md having preferred_template: modern
preferred_template updated to 'executive' in narrative.md
Resume contains 'Core Competencies' section
Each role has a 'Scope:' line with team/budget context
ATS Keyword Analysis block present
